page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Network connectivity is delivered via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, offering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench multi-core evaluations, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the average tier for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

Given its high core density and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the m8gb.24xlarge is highly efficient for parallelized workloads. Top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, database operations, and static web serving throughput makes it well-suited for high-throughput web applications and database hosting. The ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded tasks, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration will need external block storage or alternative instance types.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 96-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, a 200 Gbps baseline network interface, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. This hardware profile is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 96 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high-bandwidth networking for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
High-speed networking with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps
Top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and web serving
